title: Retry only what is safe to repeat
description: Make retried requests idempotent via idempotency keys or conditional methods so a retry never double-applies an effect.
body: Use when a request that mutates state might be retried after a timeout. Do not blindly retry a POST that creates or charges, because the first attempt may have succeeded before the response was lost, so the retry duplicates the effect. Actions: send an idempotency key the server deduplicates on, or use naturally idempotent methods (PUT with a full resource, conditional updates with ETag/If-Match); for non-idempotent operations, retry only after confirming the prior attempt did not apply. Validation signals include a test where a retried mutation with the same key applies the effect exactly once. Risk boundary: retrying a non-idempotent mutation without a dedup key is a correctness hazard; require an idempotency mechanism before enabling retries.
